S.composition: Silicate minerals such as olivine and pyroxene. Rocky and metallic layers.
S.mining_reason: Rich in iron-nickel alloys, magnesium, and precious metals. Solid rock structure favorable for anchoring.
S.india_connection: Predominantly linked to Ordinary Chondrite meteorites often found in the Deccan Traps and Rajasthan.

C.composition: Hydrated silicates, organics, and carbon. Very low albedo (dark).
C.mining_reason: High water content (ice/hydrates) which can be split into rocket fuel (H2/O2). Organic compounds for sustainable space farming.
C.india_connection: Corresponds to rare Carbonaceous Chondrites like the Vissannapeta fall in Andhra Pradesh.

X.composition: Likely purely Nickel-Iron (M-type) or enstatite chondrites (E-type).
X.mining_reason: Rich in high-purity iron, nickel, and Platinum Group Metals (PGMs) worth trillions of dollars.
X.india_connection: Metal-rich fragments found near the Lonar Crater impact site share similar metallic properties.

D.composition: Organic-rich silicates, carbon, and anhydrous silicates. Reddish in color.
D.mining_reason: Critical for understanding early solar system chemistry. Source of complex organics.
D.india_connection: Remnant materials from the proto-solar disk, similar to deep-space ice fragments found in Antarctic ice-melt studies in India.

V.composition: Basaltic crustal material similar to terrestrial volcanic rock. Rich in pyroxene.
V.mining_reason: Differentiated body materials — good for construction materials (regolith) and rare Earth elements.
V.india_connection: Direct link to HED meteorites (Howardite, Eucrite, Diogenite) which are studied at PRL, Ahmedabad.

B.composition: Sub-type of C-complex. Blue-sloped spectra indicating potential surface alterations or fine grains.
B.mining_reason: Easily accessible near-Earth targets with high hydration levels. Ideal for water extraction missions.
B.india_connection: Similar to the mineralogy of the 2013 Mukundpura CM2 carbonaceous chondrite fall.
